title: Confidence Threshold
documentation:
## Purpose
A probability cutoff below which a machine's output is considered unreliable and should be suppressed, flagged, or sent to a fallback path. Typically used in classification or decision machines.

## When to use
Set a confidence threshold when downstream processes require a minimum certainty level. Results with scores below the threshold can be routed for human review or discarded. A common starting point is 0.7–0.9 for high-precision tasks.

## Notes
Valid range: 0.0–1.0. A threshold of 0.0 accepts all results; 1.0 requires perfect certainty and will suppress most output. Pair with [Decision](https://capdag.com/browse/media:decision;json;record;textable) or classification output types that carry probability scores.
